title: drosera monticola
intro: Drosera monticola, also known as mountain sundew, is a carnivorous plant species that belongs to the Drosera genus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its leaves and roots. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving coughs and colds, and aiding in digestion, due to its ability to stimulate the immune system and promote the production of digestive enzymes. Drosera monticola can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues and other health conditions.
